heading: Certain stock purchases treated as asset acquisitions
label: 338 Certain stock purchases treated as asset acquisitions
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 338, , , made reference to a special rule relating to the effect on earnings and profits of certain distributions in partial liquidation in section 312(e), prior to repeal by .act Aug. 16, 1954, ch. 73668A Stat. 107Pub. L. 97–248, § 222(e)(4)

L. 98–369, § 712(k)(5)(D)Subsec. (h)(3)(B). , substituted in heading “under subsection (a)” for “of stock of subsidiaries” and in text “The term ‘purchase’ includes any deemed purchase under subsection (a)(2). The acquisition date for a corporation which is deemed purchased under subsection (a)(2) shall be determined under regulations prescribed by the Secretary” for “If stock in a corporation is acquired by purchase (within the meaning of subparagraph (A)) and, as a result of such acquisition, the corporation making such purchase is treated (by reason of section 318(a)) as owning stock in a 3rd corporation, the corporation making such purchase shall be treated as having purchased such stock in such 3rd corporation. The corporation making such purchase shall be treated as purchasing stock in the 3rd corporation by reason of the preceding sentence on the first day on which the purchasing corporation is considered under section 318(a) as owning such stock”.Pub. Effective Date of 1984 Amendment, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 98–369, div. A, title VII, § 712(k)(9)98 Stat. 952Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095July 18, 1984Oct. 22, 1986“(A).—In generalThe amendments made by this subsection [amending this section and sections 269 and 318 of this title] shall not apply to any qualified stock purchase (as defined in section 338(d)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954]) where the acquisition date (as defined in section 338(h)(2) of such Code) is before .September 1, 1982“(B).—Extension of time for making electionIn the case of any qualified stock purchase described in subparagraph (A), the time for making an election under section 338 of such Code shall not expire before the close of the 60th day after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”July 18, 1984Amendment by effective as if included in the provision of the Tax Equity and Fiscal Responsibility Act of 1982, , to which such amendment relates, see , set out as a note under .section 712(k) of Pub. L. 98–369Pub. L. 97–248section 715 of Pub. L. 98–369section 31 of this title

Effective Date, , , as amended by , , ; , , , provided that:Pub. L. 97–248, title II, § 224(d)96 Stat. 489Pub. L. 97–448, title III, § 306(a)(8)(B)96 Stat. 2403Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Sept. 3, 1982Jan. 12, 1983Oct. 22, 1986“(1).—In generalThe amendments made by this section [enacting this section and amending sections 168, 318, 334, 336, 337, 381, and 617 of this title] shall apply to any target corporation (within the meaning of section 338 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] as added by this section) with respect to which the acquisition date (within the meaning of such section) occurs after .August 31, 1982“(2).—Certain acquisitions beforeseptember 1, 1982If—“(A)an acquisition date (within the meaning of section 338 of such Code without regard to paragraph (5) of this subsection) occurred after , and before ,August 31, 1980September 1, 1982“(B)the target corporation (within the meaning of section 338 of such Code) is not liquidated before , andSeptember 1, 1982“(C)the purchasing corporation (within the meaning of section 338 of such Code) makes, not later than , an election under section 338 of such Code,November 15, 1982then the amendments made by this section shall apply to the acquisition of such target corporation.“(3).—Certain acquisitions of financial institutionsIn any case in which—“(A)there is, on , a binding contract to acquire control (within the meaning of section 368(c) of such Code) of any financial institution,July 22, 1982“(B)the approval of one or more regulatory authorities is required in order to complete such acquisition, and“(C)within 90 days after the date of the final approval of the last such regulatory authority granting final approval, a plan of complete liquidation of such financial institution is adopted,then the purchasing corporation may elect not to have the amendments made by this section apply to the acquisition pursuant to such contract.“(4)Extension of time for making elections; revocation of elections.—“(A).—ExtensionThe time for making an election under section 338 of such Code shall not expire before the close of .February 28, 1983“(B).—RevocationAny election made under section 338 of such Code may be revoked by the purchasing corporation if revoked before .March 1, 1983“(5)Rules for acquisitions described in paragraph (2).—“(A).—In generalFor purposes of applying section 338 of such Code with respect to any acquisition described in paragraph (2)—“(i)the date selected under subparagraph (B) of this paragraph shall be treated as the acquisition date,“(ii)a rule similar to the last sentence of section 334(b)(2) of such Code (as in effect on ) shall apply, andAugust 31, 1982“(iii)subsections (e), (f), and (i) of such section 338, and paragraphs (4), (6), (8), and (9) of subsection (h) of such section 338, shall not apply.“(B).—Selection of acquisition date by purchasing corporationThe purchasing corporation may select any date for purposes of subparagraph (A)(i) if such date—“(i)is after the later of , or the acquisition date (within the meaning of section 338 of such Code without regard to this paragraph), andJune 30, 1982“(ii)is on or before the date on which the election described in paragraph (2)(C) is made.”

Savings ProvisionFor provisions that nothing in amendment by be construed to affect treatment of certain transactions occurring, property acquired, or items of income, loss, deduction, or credit taken into account prior to , for purposes of determining liability for tax for periods ending after , see , set out as a note under .section 401(d)(1)(D)(vii) of Pub. L. 115–141section 401(e) of Pub. L. 115–141section 23 of this titleMar. 23, 2018Mar. 23, 2018

Treatment of Certain Corporation Organized onFebruary 22, 1983, , , provided that:Pub. L. 99–514, title XVIII, § 1804(e)(9)100 Stat. 2804Oct. 22, 1986“In the case of a Rhode Island corporation which was organized on , and which on —February 22, 1983February 25, 1983“(A)purchased the stock of another corporation,“(B)filed an election under section 338(g) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 with respect to such purchase, and“(C)merged into the acquired corporation,such purchase of stock shall be considered as made by the acquiring corporation, such election shall be valid, and the acquiring corporation shall be considered a purchasing corporation for purposes of section 338 of such Code without regard to the duration of the existence of the acquiring corporation.”

Special Rules for Deemed Purchases under Prior Law, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 98–369, div. A, title VII, § 712(k)(10)98 Stat. 953Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095July 18, 1984Oct. 22, 1986“If, before , a corporation was treated as making a qualified stock purchase (as defined in section 338(d)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954]), but would not be so treated under the amendments made by paragraphs (5) and (6) [amending subsec. (h) and ] of this subsection, the amendments made by such paragraphs shall not apply to such purchase unless such corporation elects (at such time and in such manner as the Secretary of the Treasury or his delegate may by regulations prescribe) to have the amendments made by such paragraphs apply.”October 20, 1983section 318(b)(4) of this title

Exception for Stock Purchases in Contemplation of Target Corporation as Member of Affiliated Group, , , as amended by , , ; , , , provided that:Pub. L. 97–448, title III, § 306(a)(8)(A)(ii)96 Stat. 2402Pub. L. 98–369, div. A, title VII, § 722(a)(3)98 Stat. 973Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Jan. 12, 1983July 18, 1984Oct. 22, 1986“If—“(I)any portion of a qualified stock purchase is pursuant to a binding contract entered into on or after , and on or before the date of the enactment of this Act [], andSeptember 1, 1982Jan. 12, 1983“(II)the purchasing corporation establishes by clear and convincing evidence that such contract was negotiated on the contemplation that, with respect to the deemed sale under section 338 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954], the target corporation would be treated as a member of the affiliated group which includes the selling corporation,then the amendment made by clause (i) [amending subsec. (h)] shall not apply to such qualified stock purchase.”

Effective Date, , , as amended by , , ; , , , provided that:Pub. L. 97–248, title II, § 224(d)96 Stat. 489Pub. L. 97–448, title III, § 306(a)(8)(B)96 Stat. 2403Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Sept. 3, 1982Jan. 12, 1983Oct. 22, 1986“(1).—In generalThe amendments made by this section [enacting this section and amending sections 168, 318, 334, 336, 337, 381, and 617 of this title] shall apply to any target corporation (within the meaning of section 338 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] as added by this section) with respect to which the acquisition date (within the meaning of such section) occurs after .August 31, 1982“(2).—Certain acquisitions beforeseptember 1, 1982If—“(A)an acquisition date (within the meaning of section 338 of such Code without regard to paragraph (5) of this subsection) occurred after , and before ,August 31, 1980September 1, 1982“(B)the target corporation (within the meaning of section 338 of such Code) is not liquidated before , andSeptember 1, 1982“(C)the purchasing corporation (within the meaning of section 338 of such Code) makes, not later than , an election under section 338 of such Code,November 15, 1982then the amendments made by this section shall apply to the acquisition of such target corporation.“(3).—Certain acquisitions of financial institutionsIn any case in which—“(A)there is, on , a binding contract to acquire control (within the meaning of section 368(c) of such Code) of any financial institution,July 22, 1982“(B)the approval of one or more regulatory authorities is required in order to complete such acquisition, and“(C)within 90 days after the date of the final approval of the last such regulatory authority granting final approval, a plan of complete liquidation of such financial institution is adopted,then the purchasing corporation may elect not to have the amendments made by this section apply to the acquisition pursuant to such contract.“(4)Extension of time for making elections; revocation of elections.—“(A).—ExtensionThe time for making an election under section 338 of such Code shall not expire before the close of .February 28, 1983“(B).—RevocationAny election made under section 338 of such Code may be revoked by the purchasing corporation if revoked before .March 1, 1983“(5)Rules for acquisitions described in paragraph (2).—“(A).—In generalFor purposes of applying section 338 of such Code with respect to any acquisition described in paragraph (2)—“(i)the date selected under subparagraph (B) of this paragraph shall be treated as the acquisition date,“(ii)a rule similar to the last sentence of section 334(b)(2) of such Code (as in effect on ) shall apply, andAugust 31, 1982“(iii)subsections (e), (f), and (i) of such section 338, and paragraphs (4), (6), (8), and (9) of subsection (h) of such section 338, shall not apply.“(B).—Selection of acquisition date by purchasing corporationThe purchasing corporation may select any date for purposes of subparagraph (A)(i) if such date—“(i)is after the later of , or the acquisition date (within the meaning of section 338 of such Code without regard to this paragraph), andJune 30, 1982“(ii)is on or before the date on which the election described in paragraph (2)(C) is made.”

children.text: shall be treated as having sold all of its assets at the close of the acquisition date at fair market value in a single transaction, and
shall be treated as a new corporation which purchased all of the assets referred to in paragraph (1) as of the beginning of the day after the acquisition date.

children.heading: Basis of assets after deemed purchase
children.label: b Basis of assets after deemed purchase
A
children.text: the grossed-up basis of the purchasing corporation’s recently purchased stock, and

children.label: B
children.text: the basis of the purchasing corporation’s nonrecently purchased stock.

children.heading: Adjustment for liabilities and other relevant items
children.label: 2 Adjustment for liabilities and other relevant items
children.text: The amount described in paragraph (1) shall be adjusted under regulations prescribed by the Secretary for liabilities of the target corporation and other relevant items.

children.heading: Election to step-up the basis of certain target stock
children.label: 3 Election to step-up the basis of certain target stock

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, the basis of the purchasing corporation’s nonrecently purchased stock shall be the basis amount determined under subparagraph (B) of this paragraph if the purchasing corporation makes an election to recognize gain as if such stock were sold on the acquisition date for an amount equal to the basis amount determined under subparagraph (B).

children.label: i
children.text: the numerator of which is the percentage of stock (by value) in the target corporation attributable to the purchasing corporation’s nonrecently purchased stock, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the denominator of which is 100 percent minus the percentage referred to in clause (i).

children.label: A
children.text: the numerator of which is 100 percent, minus the percentage of stock (by value) in the target corporation attributable to the purchasing corporation’s nonrecently purchased stock, and

children.label: B
children.text: the denominator of which is the percentage of stock (by value) in the target corporation attributable to the purchasing corporation’s recently purchased stock.

children.heading: Allocation among assets
children.label: 5 Allocation among assets
children.text: The amount determined under paragraphs (1) and (2) shall be allocated among the assets of the target corporation under regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Definitions of recently purchased stock and nonrecently purchased stock
children.label: 6 Definitions of recently purchased stock and nonrecently purchased stock

children.heading: Recently purchased stock
children.label: A Recently purchased stock
children.text: The term “recently purchased stock” means any stock in the target corporation which is held by the purchasing corporation on the acquisition date and which was purchased by such corporation during the 12-month acquisition period.

children.heading: Nonrecently purchased stock
children.label: B Nonrecently purchased stock
children.text: The term “nonrecently purchased stock” means any stock in the target corporation which is held by the purchasing corporation on the acquisition date and which is not recently purchased stock.

children.heading: Repealed. , , ]Pub. L. 99–514, title VI, § 631(b)(2)100 Stat. 2272Oct. 22, 1986
children.label: c Repealed. , , ]Pub. L. 99–514, title VI, § 631(b)(2)100 Stat. 2272Oct. 22, 1986

children.heading: Purchasing corporation; target corporation; qualified stock purchase
children.label: d Purchasing corporation; target corporation; qualified stock purchase

children.heading: Purchasing corporation
children.label: 1 Purchasing corporation
children.text: The term “purchasing corporation” means any corporation which makes a qualified stock purchase of stock of another corporation.

children.heading: Target corporation
children.label: 2 Target corporation
children.text: The term “target corporation” means any corporation the stock of which is acquired by another corporation in a qualified stock purchase.

children.heading: Qualified stock purchase
children.label: 3 Qualified stock purchase
children.text: The term “qualified stock purchase” means any transaction or series of transactions in which stock (meeting the requirements of section 1504(a)(2)) of 1 corporation is acquired by another corporation by purchase during the 12-month acquisition period.

children.heading: Deemed election where purchasing corporation acquires asset of target corporation
children.label: e Deemed election where purchasing corporation acquires asset of target corporation

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A purchasing corporation shall be treated as having made an election under this section with respect to any target corporation if, at any time during the consistency period, it acquires any asset of the target corporation (or a target affiliate).

children.label: A
children.text: such acquisition is pursuant to a sale by the target corporation (or the target affiliate) in the ordinary course of its trade or business,

children.label: B
children.text: the basis of the property acquired is determined wholly by reference to the adjusted basis of such property in the hands of the person from whom acquired,

children.label: C
children.text: such acquisition was before , orSeptember 1, 1982

children.label: D
children.text: such acquisition is described in regulations prescribed by the Secretary and meets such conditions as such regulations may provide.

children.heading: Anti-avoidance rule
children.label: 3 Anti-avoidance rule
children.text: Whenever necessary to carry out the purpose of this subsection and subsection (f), the Secretary may treat stock acquisitions which are pursuant to a plan and which meet the requirements of section 1504(a)(2) as qualified stock purchases.

children.heading: Consistency required for all stock acquisitions from same affiliated group
children.label: f Consistency required for all stock acquisitions from same affiliated group

children.text: any election under this section with respect to the first such purchase shall apply to each other such purchase, and
no election may be made under this section with respect to the second or subsequent such purchase if such an election was not made with respect to the first such purchase.

children.heading: When made
children.label: 1 When made
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in regulations, an election under this section shall be made not later than the 15th day of the 9th month beginning after the month in which the acquisition date occurs.

children.heading: Manner
children.label: 2 Manner
children.text: An election by the purchasing corporation under this section shall be made in such manner as the Secretary shall by regulations prescribe.

children.heading: Election irrevocable
children.label: 3 Election irrevocable
children.text: An election by a purchasing corporation under this section, once made, shall be irrevocable.

children.heading: 12-month acquisition period
children.label: 1 12-month acquisition period
children.text: The term “12-month acquisition period” means the 12-month period beginning with the date of the first acquisition by purchase of stock included in a qualified stock purchase (or, if any of such stock was acquired in an acquisition which is a purchase by reason of subparagraph (C) of paragraph (3), the date on which the acquiring corporation is first considered under section 318(a) (other than paragraph (4) thereof) as owning stock owned by the corporation from which such acquisition was made).

children.heading: Acquisition date
children.label: 2 Acquisition date
children.text: The term “acquisition date” means, with respect to any corporation, the first day on which there is a qualified stock purchase with respect to the stock of such corporation.

children.label: i
children.text: the basis of the stock in the hands of the purchasing corporation is not determined (I) in whole or in part by reference to the adjusted basis of such stock in the hands of the person from whom acquired, or (II) under section 1014(a) (relating to property acquired from a decedent),

children.label: ii
children.text: the stock is not acquired in an exchange to which section 351, 354, 355, or 356 applies and is not acquired in any other transaction described in regulations in which the transferor does not recognize the entire amount of the gain or loss realized on the transaction, and

children.label: iii
children.text: the stock is not acquired from a person the ownership of whose stock would, under section 318(a) (other than paragraph (4) thereof), be attributed to the person acquiring such stock.

children.heading: Deemed purchase under subsection (a)
children.label: B Deemed purchase under subsection (a)
children.text: The term “purchase” includes any deemed purchase under subsection (a)(2). The acquisition date for a corporation which is deemed purchased under subsection (a)(2) shall be determined under regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Certain stock acquisitions from related corporations
children.label: C Certain stock acquisitions from related corporations

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Clause (iii) of subparagraph (A) shall not apply to an acquisition of stock from a related corporation if at least 50 percent in value of the stock of such related corporation was acquired by purchase (within the meaning of subparagraphs (A) and (B)).

children.label: I
children.text: made a qualified stock purchase of stock of the related corporation, and

children.label: II
children.text: made an election under this section (or is treated under subsection (e) as having made such an election) with respect to such qualified stock purchase.

children.heading: Related corporation defined
children.label: iii Related corporation defined
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, a corporation is a related corporation if stock owned by such corporation is treated (under section 318(a) other than paragraph (4) thereof) as owned by the corporation acquiring the stock.

children.label: i
children.text: the 1-year period before the beginning of the 12-month acquisition period for the target corporation,

children.label: ii
children.text: such acquisition period (up to and including the acquisition date), and

children.label: iii
children.text: the 1-year period beginning on the day after the acquisition date.

children.heading: Extension where there is plan
children.label: B Extension where there is plan
children.text: The period referred to in subparagraph (A) shall also include any period during which the Secretary determines that there was in effect a plan to make a qualified stock purchase plus 1 or more other qualified stock purchases (or asset acquisitions described in subsection (e)) with respect to the target corporation or any target affiliate.

children.heading: Affiliated group
children.label: 5 Affiliated group
children.text: The term “affiliated group” has the meaning given to such term by section 1504(a) (determined without regard to the exceptions contained in section 1504(b)).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A corporation shall be treated as a target affiliate of the target corporation if each of such corporations was, at any time during so much of the consistency period as ends on the acquisition date of the target corporation, a member of an affiliated group which had the same common parent.

children.label: i
children.text: the term “target affiliate” does not include a foreign corporation or a DISC, and

children.label: ii
children.text: stock held by a target affiliate in a foreign corporation or a domestic corporation which is a DISC or described in section 1248(e) shall be excluded from the operation of this section.

children.heading: Acquisitions by affiliated group treated as made by 1 corporation
children.label: 8 Acquisitions by affiliated group treated as made by 1 corporation
children.text: Except as provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary, stock and asset acquisitions made by members of the same affiliated group shall be treated as made by 1 corporation.

children.heading: Target not treated as member of affiliated group
children.label: 9 Target not treated as member of affiliated group
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in paragraph (10) or in regulations prescribed under this paragraph, the target corporation shall not be treated as a member of an affiliated group with respect to the sale described in subsection (a)(1).

children.heading: Elective recognition of gain or loss by target corporation, together with nonrecognition of gain or loss on stock sold by selling consolidated group
children.label: 10 Elective recognition of gain or loss by target corporation, together with nonrecognition of gain or loss on stock sold by selling consolidated group
i
children.text: the target corporation was, before the transaction, a member of the selling consolidated group, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the target corporation recognizes gain or loss with respect to the transaction as if it sold all of its assets in a single transaction,

children.label: i
children.text: includes the target corporation, and

children.label: ii
children.text: files a consolidated return.

children.heading: Information required to be furnished to the Secretary
children.label: C Information required to be furnished to the Secretary
i
children.text: The amount allocated under subsection (b)(5) to goodwill or going concern value.

children.label: ii
children.text: Any modification of the amount described in clause (i).

children.label: iii
children.text: Any other information as the Secretary deems necessary to carry out the provisions of this paragraph.

children.heading: Elective formula for determining fair market value
children.label: 11 Elective formula for determining fair market value
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a)(1), fair market value may be determined on the basis of a formula provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary which takes into account liabilities and other relevant items.

children.heading: Tax on deemed sale not taken into account for estimated tax purposes
children.label: 13 Tax on deemed sale not taken into account for estimated tax purposes
children.text: For purposes of section 6655, tax attributable to the sale described in subsection (a)(1) shall not be taken into account. The preceding sentence shall not apply with respect to a qualified stock purchase for which an election is made under paragraph (10).

children.heading: Combined deemed sale return
children.label: 15 Combined deemed sale return
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, a combined deemed sale return may be filed by all target corporations acquired by a purchasing corporation on the same acquisition date if such target corporations were members of the same selling consolidated group (as defined in subparagraph (B) of paragraph (10)).

children.heading: Coordination with foreign tax credit provisions
children.label: 16 Coordination with foreign tax credit provisions
children.text: Except as provided in regulations, this section shall not apply for purposes of determining the source or character of any item for purposes of subpart A of part III of subchapter N of this chapter (relating to foreign tax credit). The preceding sentence shall not apply to any gain to the extent such gain is includible in gross income as a dividend under section 1248 (determined without regard to any deemed sale under this section by a foreign corporation).

regulations to ensure that the purpose of this section to require consistency of treatment of stock and asset sales and purchases may not be circumvented through the use of any provision of law or regulations (including the consolidated return regulations) and
regulations providing for the coordination of the provisions of this section with the provision of this title relating to foreign corporations and their shareholders.
